macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Elevated at $106 Brent Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices remain high with Brent at $106 due to escalating tensions in the Middle East; global markets closed cautiously as investors react to hotter-than-expected US inflation data.

items.title: US Markets Mixed on Hot CPI Data, Nasdaq Falls 186 Points
items.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped from highs with tech stocks declining after CPI exceeded expectations, raising fears of prolonged high Fed rates; Dow rose slightly by 56 points on healthcare support.

items.title: Trump Faces Backlash Over Inflation Surge to 3.8%
items.summary: Inflation hit 3.8% in April driven by Iran conflict pushing gas above $4.50/gallon; polls show 77% of Americans blame Trump's policies for higher living costs, with wages lagging prices.

items.title: Kevin Warsh Advances Toward Fed Chair Role
items.summary: Kevin Warsh cleared a key Senate vote, nearing confirmation as Fed Chairman to replace Jerome Powell amid rising inflation and wholesale prices up 6% YoY.

items.title: US 10-Year Yield Climbs to 4.45% Post-CPI
items.summary: Treasury yields and dollar rose after CPI data came in higher than expected, increasing bets on delayed Fed rate cuts or potential tightening.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $80K Despite Hot US Inflation Data
items.summary: Bitcoin maintained its position above the $80,000 mark despite hotter-than-expected U.S. CPI data, trading at $81,117 with a modest 0.17% decline over 24 hours. The resilience signals strong buyer confidence and marks a behavioral shift, as Bitcoin historically declined after CPI releases.

items.title: XRP Overtakes Bitcoin and Ether in South Korean Trading Volumes
items.summary: XRP has surpassed Bitcoin and Ethereum in trading volumes on South Korea's largest exchanges Upbit and Bithumb, with XRP/KRW ranking as the most traded pair on Upbit. This surge echoes historical retail front-running patterns that often precede sharper price expansions.

items.title: Ethereum Only Top-10 Crypto Down This Week
items.summary: Ethereum is the only top-10 cryptocurrency trading in negative territory this week, with the ETH/BTC ratio falling to a 10-month low of 0.02835. This underperformance contrasts with Bitcoin holding above $80,000 and Solana gaining nearly 10%.

items.title: Bitcoin Faces $400M Liquidations While Testing Critical Resistance
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced over $400M in liquidations within 24 hours as Bitcoin tested a critical resistance zone. Bitcoin has climbed 40% from February lows, with expert forecasts suggesting potential reaches of $100,000 to $230,000 in 2026.

items.title: U.S. PPI Hits 6% — Markets Price Rate Hike by December
items.summary: U.S. Producer Price Index reached 6%, prompting markets to price in a potential rate hike by December. Bitcoin traded between $79,481 and $81,325 over the past 24 hours amid the macroeconomic data.

items.title: Bitcoin Daily Transactions Surge 116% Since Start of May
items.summary: On-chain activity shows Bitcoin daily transactions have surged 116% since the beginning of May, reflecting notable increases in user engagement. Additionally, over 3 million ETH have been withdrawn from exchanges since early May, potentially reducing selling pressure.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Agent View for Claude Code
items.summary: Anthropic has introduced Agent View in Claude Code, enabling users to run 10+ sessions in parallel from one screen, with shortcuts and dispatch tricks that save hours of work. The feature aims to shift from babysitting to managing AI agents effectively.
items.source: YouTube - No Priors: AI, Machine Learning, Tech, & Startups

items.title: Lex Fridman Discusses Path to AGI and AI Automation Trends
items.summary: In a recent podcast, discussions highlight rapid advancements in tools like Claude Code for building websites or analyzing data in hours, with predictions of massive software automation by year-end, including complex tasks like reinforcement learning across GPU clusters.

items.title: Insights from Implementing LLM Architectures from Scratch
items.summary: A new talk explores implementing modern LLM architectures in Python and PyTorch, covering attention variants, normalization, KV cache optimizations, and trends from recent open-weight models to deepen understanding of LLM internals.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: SEGA Cancels Massive 'Super Game' Project
items.summary: SEGA has reportedly canceled its ambitious 'Super Game' project, as revealed in recent gaming news updates. The cancellation comes amid other industry shifts like Nintendo's price increase announcement.

items.title: Nintendo Announces Price Increase Ahead of Summer
items.summary: Nintendo has made an interesting strategic move by announcing a price increase for its products heading into the summer period. This development is part of broader gaming industry updates.

items.title: Batman Beyond Game Leak Causes Online Buzz
items.summary: A major leak related to a potential Batman Beyond game has exploded online, sparking widespread discussion in the gaming community. Details emerged in today's gaming news roundup.

items.title: Saros Reportedly Selling Slower Than Returnal
items.summary: New sales data indicates that Saros is underperforming compared to Returnal, highlighting challenges in the gaming market. This was discussed in a recent industry analysis.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony Launches Xperia 1 VIII with Wild Telephoto Camera and New Design
items.summary: Sony has released the Xperia 1 VIII featuring an innovative telephoto camera system and redesigned aesthetics, though it comes with a notably high price tag.

items.title: Samsung Rolls Out One UI 9 Beta for Galaxy S26 with Android 17
items.summary: Samsung has launched the first One UI 9 beta update based on Android 17 for the Galaxy S26 series, available in six markets for eligible devices.

items.title: iOS 26.5 Introduces Cross-Platform Encrypted RCS Messaging
items.summary: Apple's iOS 26.5 update enables default end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging between iPhones and Android devices, enhancing cross-platform communication with some limitations.

items.title: eBay Rejects GameStop's $56B Unsolicited Acquisition Offer
items.summary: eBay has dismissed GameStop's $56 billion bid as 'neither credible nor attractive,' halting the proposed takeover attempt.

items.title: Instructure Strikes Deal with ShinyHunters Hackers Before Ransomware Deadline
items.summary: Edtech company Instructure, owner of Canvas, reached an agreement with the ShinyHunters hacking group to secure stolen data ahead of a ransomware deadline.

items.title: Oppo Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s Set for India Launch Next Week
items.summary: Oppo is preparing to bring its flagship Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s smartphones to the Indian market next week.
